channel.title: Data Breach & Ransomware Aggregator
channel.description: Real-time data breach notifications from state registries, federal sources, and ransomware trackers

item.title: Kerkering, Barberio & Co., Certified Public Accountants
item.description:
Kerkering, Barberio & Co., Certified Public Accountants reported a state registry affecting 4179 records in Sarasota, FL.
📊 Records Affected: 4179 | 📍 Location: Sarasota, FL | 📰 Source: Maine AG | 🏷️ Type: State Registry

item.title: Officials worry Salt Typhoon apathy is killing momentum for tougher telecom security rules
item.description:
Cyber officials lamented Wednesday that its a challenge to make the wider population appreciate the gravity of the threat the hacking group presents. The post Officials worry Salt Typhoon apathy is killing momentum for tougher telecom security rules appeared first on CyberScoop.
📰 Source: CyberScoop | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Stryker attack highlights nebulous nature of Iranian cyber activity amid joint U.S.-Israel conflict
item.description:
It’s been difficult early on to separate signal from noise, even if the attack on the medical device maker looks like a qualified success for the attackers. The post Stryker attack highlights nebulous nature of Iranian cyber activity amid joint U.S.-Israel conflict appeared first on CyberScoop.
📰 Source: CyberScoop | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Russian Threat Actor Sednit Resurfaces With Sophisticated Toolkit
item.description:
After several years of using simple implants, the Russia-affiliated actor is back with two new sophisticated malware tools.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Xygeni GitHub Action Compromised Via Tag Poison
item.description:
Attackers operated an active C2 implant for up to a week and compromised AppSec vendor Xygeni's xygeni/xygeni-action in that time.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: INC Ransomware Group Holds Healthcare Hostage in Oceania
item.description:
Government agencies, emergency clinics, and others in Australia, New Zealand, and Tonga have had serious run-ins with the prolific ransomware outfit.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Iran MOIS Colludes With Criminals to Boost Cyberattacks
item.description:
Iranian APTs have long pretended to be cybercriminal groups. Now they're working with actual cybercriminal groups.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Most Google Cloud Attacks Start With Bug Exploitation
item.description:
Forget stolen credentials and misconfigurations; AI means vulnerability exploits that beat patching cycles are the top cause of compromises in the cloud.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Will AI Save Consumers From Smartphone-Based Phishing Attacks?
item.description:
Sophisticated phishing attacks are bypassing on-device protections with troubling frequency, making it more critical than ever for users to protect themselves from potential threats, new research from Omdia shows.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Cyberattackers Don't Care About Good Causes
item.description:
Sightline Security's founder and advisory board discuss how cybersecurity poses significant problems for nonprofits and suggest ways the industry can help.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: The Data Gap: Why Nonprofit Cyber Incidents Go Underreported
item.description:
Threat actors target nonprofits due to security gaps and highly coveted information, but a lack of sufficient data makes it difficult to grasp the entire picture.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Onyx Security Launches With $40 Million in Funding
item.description:
The startup is building a control pane to help organizations oversee autonomous AI agents and rapidly adopt them. The post Onyx Security Launches With $40 Million in Funding appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Iran-Linked Hacker Attack on Stryker Disrupted Manufacturing and Shipping
item.description:
Evidence indicates that the attackers leveraged existing endpoint management software rather than malware to wipe devices. The post Iran-Linked Hacker Attack on Stryker Disrupted Manufacturing and Shipping appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Bold Security Emerges From Stealth With $40 Million in Funding
item.description:
The startup relies on AI to turn devices into active agents that understand users’ actions and provide protection in real time. The post Bold Security Emerges From Stealth With $40 Million in Funding appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Iran-Linked Hackers Take Aim at US and Other Targets, Raising Risk of Cyberattacks During War
item.description:
Pro-Iranian hackers are targeting sites in the Middle East and starting to stretch into the United States during the war, raising the risk of American defense contractors, power stations and water plants. The post Iran-Linked Hackers Take Aim at US and Other Targets, Raising Risk of Cyberattacks During War appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: In Other News: N8n Flaw Exploited, Slopoly Malware, Interpol Cybercrime Crackdown
item.description:
Other noteworthy stories that might have slipped under the radar: Telus Digital data breach, vulnerabilities in Linux AppArmor allow root privileges, US defense contractor behind Coruna exploits. The post In Other News: N8n Flaw Exploited, Slopoly Malware, Interpol Cybercrime Crackdown appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Starbucks Data Breach Impacts Employees
item.description:
Starbucks said the incident involved phishing attacks targeting an employee portal, affecting hundreds. The post Starbucks Data Breach Impacts Employees appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Critical HPE AOS-CX Vulnerability Allows Admin Password Resets
item.description:
The vulnerability can be exploited remotely, without authentication, to circumvent existing authentication controls. The post Critical HPE AOS-CX Vulnerability Allows Admin Password Resets appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Loblaw Data Breach Impacts Customer Information
item.description:
Personal information such as names, email addresses, and phone numbers was accessed by hackers. The post Loblaw Data Breach Impacts Customer Information appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Exclusive: New data shows increase in FBI searches of Americans’ data last year
item.description:
The number of FBI searches of data collected through the surveillance program known as Section 702 of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act (FISA) between December 2024 to November 2025 rose to 7,413 from 5,518 the previous year.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: US, Europol disrupt SocksEscort network that exploited thousands of residential routers
item.description:
The SocksEscort proxy network allowed cybercriminals to purchase access to routers infected with malware, which they used to conceal their location and IP addresses.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Ransomware incident responder gave info to BlackCat cybercriminals during negotiations, DOJ alleges
item.description:
U.S. prosecutors accused an incident responder of conducting cyberattacks and helping ransomware gangs negotiate higher payouts from the same victims he was working for.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: New York cyber regulations for water organizations to take effect in 2027
item.description:
The new rules for water and wastewater entities in New York include mandatory cybersecurity training for certified operators, incident response plans and reporting requirements.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: ‘Starkiller’ Phishing Service Proxies Real Login Pages, MFA
item.description:
Most phishing websites are little more than static copies of login pages for popular online destinations, and they are often quickly taken down by anti-abuse activists and security firms. But a stealthy new phishing-as-a-service offering lets customers sidestep both of these pitfalls: It uses cleverly disguised links to load the target brand's real website, and then acts as a relay between the target and the legitimate site -- forwarding the victim's username, password and multi-factor authentic
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: How AI Assistants are Moving the Security Goalposts
item.description:
AI-based assistants or "agents" -- autonomous programs that have access to the user's computer, files, online services and can automate virtually any task -- are growing in popularity with developers and IT workers. But as so many eyebrow-raising headlines over the past few weeks have shown, these powerful and assertive new tools are rapidly shifting the security priorities for organizations, while blurring the lines between data and code, trusted co-worker and insider threat, ninja hacker and n
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Iran-Backed Hackers Claim Wiper Attack on Medtech Firm Stryker
item.description:
A hacktivist group with links to Iran's intelligence agencies is claiming responsibility for a data-wiping attack against Stryker, a global medical technology company based in Michigan. News reports out of Ireland, Stryker's largest hub outside of the United States, said the company sent home more than 5,000 workers there today. Meanwhile, a voicemail message at Stryker's main U.S. headquarters says the company is currently experiencing a building emergency.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.description:
Verification alert Listings attributed to COINBASECARTEL have been reported as including unverified or fabricated victim...
🎭 Threat Actor: COINBASECARTEL | 📰 Source: Red Packet Security | 🏷️ Type: Ransomware

🎭 Threat Actor: QILIN | 📰 Source: Red Packet Security | 🏷️ Type: Ransomware

item.title: The rise of teen hackers ‘makes for a good headline’, but cyber crime activities peak later in life
item.description:
Emma Woollacott reports: While much emphasis has been placed on the rise of youth cyber crime over the last two years, new research shows hacker activity peaks much later. Orange Cyberdefense looked at the numbers and found that it’s actually thirty- and forty-somethings that are the greatest threat. The company’s intelligence team analyzed 418 publicly announced law enforcement activities... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Police Scotland fined £66k for extracting and sharing mobile phone data
item.description:
Scottish Legal News reports: The Information Commissioner’s Office (ICO) issued the fine and reprimand after finding that a series of data protection failures resulted in the excessive collection, handling and unlawful disclosure of sensitive personal information. The data protection authority says the case highlights key data protection practices that all police services and criminal justice... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Handala claims responsibility for attack on medical device maker Stryker
item.description:
A.J. Vicens and Christy Santhosh report: An Iranian-linked hacking group on Wednesday claimed ‌responsibility for a destructive cyberattack on U.S.-based medical device and services provider Stryker, according to messages posted to the group’s Telegram channel. The Michigan-based company, with 56,000 employees and operations in 61 countries, said in a filing with the SEC that the... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Lotte Card fined 9.6 billion won for leaking users’ social registration numbers
item.description:
Korea JoongAng Daily reports: Lotte Card was fined 9.6 billion won ($6.5 million) by the Personal Information Protection Commission (PIPC) after 450,000 users’ social registration numbers were leaked. The PIPC decided to impose an administrative fine of 9.62 billion won and a penalty of 4.8 million won on Lotte Card for violations of the Personal Information... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Bell Ambulance data breach impacted over 238,000 people
item.description:
Pierluigi Paganini reports: Nearly 238,000 individuals are impacted by a February 2025 Bell Ambulance data breach. Bell Ambulance is a U.S.-based emergency medical services provider offering ambulance transport, paramedic care, and patient support. It serves communities with urgent medical response, interfacility transfers, and non-emergency transport, focusing on patient safety and timely care. On February 13,... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: China’s CERT warns OpenClaw can inflict nasty wounds
item.description:
Simon Sharwood reports: China’s National Computer Network Emergency Response Technical Team has warned locals that the OpenClaw agentic AI tool poses significant security risks. In a Tuesday post to its WeChat account, the CERT warned that OpenClaw has “extremely weak default security configuration” and must therefore be handled with extreme care. The CERT is worried that attackers... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Telus Digital confirms breach after ShinyHunters claims 1 petabyte data theft
item.description:
Lawrence Abrams reports: Canadian business process outsourcing giant Telus Digital has confirmed it suffered a security incident after threat actors claimed to have stolen nearly 1 petabyte of data from the company in a multi-month breach. Telus Digital is the digital services and business process outsourcing (BPO) arm of Canadian telecommunications provider Telus, providing customer support,... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: The Broken Records: tracing the human cost of the 2022 British MoD leak
item.description:
Harvey Depledge-Kittle writes: In February 2022, a Ministry of Defence data breach exposed sensitive personal information relating to approximately 18,700 Afghan nationals who had assisted UK forces and applied for protection. The breach was not publicly disclosed for nearly two years. From September 2023, a High Court super-injunction prohibited reporting on the breach and, initially,... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: 45,000 malicious IP addresses taken down in international cyber operation
item.description:
An international cybercrime operation targeting phishing, malware and ransomware has taken down more than 45,000 malicious IP addresses and servers. Law enforcement from 72 countries and territories took part in Operation Synergia III (18 July 2025 – 31 January 2026), coordinated by INTERPOL. The operation led to the arrest of 94 people, with another 110... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Ransomware incident responder gave info to BlackCat cybercriminals during negotiations, DOJ alleges
item.description:
It’s not like threat actors weren’t telling some of us about rogue negotiators. They were. Now I wonder how many other journalists also disbelieved the threat actors when they were telling the truth. Jonathan Greig reports: The Justice Department is accusing an incident responder of conducting cyberattacks and helping ransomware gangs negotiate higher payouts from... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Dozens of Lawsuits Filed in Response to Kettering Health Ransomware Attack
item.description:
Dozens of lawsuits have been filed against Kettering Health in response to a 2025 ransomware attack involving data theft and […] The post Dozens of Lawsuits Filed in Response to Kettering Health Ransomware Attack appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Data Breaches Reported by Centerwell & Lakeside Pediatric & Adolescent Medicine
item.description:
Centerwell, a provider of senior healthcare services in 30 U.S. states, has experienced a cyberattack and data breach. Lakeside Pediatric […] The post Data Breaches Reported by Centerwell & Lakeside Pediatric & Adolescent Medicine appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: ID Care & CommuniCare Announce Data Breaches
item.description:
ID Care in New Jersey and Barrio Comprehensive Family Health Care Center (CommuniCare) in Texas have confirmed that patients’ personal […] The post ID Care & CommuniCare Announce Data Breaches appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Proliance Surgeons Settles Data Breach Litigation for $4,450,000
item.description:
The Seattle, Washington-based surgical group, Proliance Surgeons, has agreed to a settlement to resolve class action litigation over a February […] The post Proliance Surgeons Settles Data Breach Litigation for $4,450,000 appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Iran Linked Hacking Group Wipes Data of U.S. Medical Device Manufacturer
item.description:
Stryker, a U.S. medical device and medical equipment manufacturer based in Portage, Michigan, is dealing with a cyberattack linked to […] The post Iran Linked Hacking Group Wipes Data of U.S. Medical Device Manufacturer appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Third Ransomware Negotiator Charged Over Involvement with BlackCat Ransomware Group
item.description:
Another former employee of DigitalMint has been accused of involvement with the ALPHV/Blackcat ransomware group while working as a ransomware […] The post Third Ransomware Negotiator Charged Over Involvement with BlackCat Ransomware Group appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Orthopaedic Institute of Western Kentucky Patients Affected by Vendor Data Breach
item.description:
Orthopaedic Institute of Western Kentucky has notified patients that their PHI was compromised in two security incidents at their managed […] The post Orthopaedic Institute of Western Kentucky Patients Affected by Vendor Data Breach appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Long Island Plastic Surgical Group Settles Class Action Lawsuit Over BlackCat Ransomware Attack
item.description:
A consolidated class action lawsuit against Long Island Plastic Surgical Group, P.C has been resolved with a $2,600,000 settlement. Legal action […] The post Long Island Plastic Surgical Group Settles Class Action Lawsuit Over BlackCat Ransomware Attack appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: England Hockey investigating ransomware data breach
item.description:
England Hockey, the governing body for field hockey in England, is investigating a potential data breach after the AiLock ransomware gang listed it as a victim on its data leak site. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Canadian retail giant Loblaw notifies customers of data breach
item.description:
Still, out of an abundance of caution, Loblaw says it has automatically logged out all customers from their accounts. Account holders who need to access the company's digital services will have to log in again. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Google fixes two new Chrome zero-days exploited in attacks
item.description:
Google has released emergency security updates to patch two high-severity Chrome vulnerabilities exploited in zero-day attacks. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Starbucks discloses data breach affecting hundreds of employees
item.description:
Starbucks has disclosed a data breach affecting hundreds of employees after threat actors gained access to their Starbucks Partner Central accounts. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Poland's nuclear research centre targeted by cyberattack
item.description:
Poland's National Centre for Nuclear Research (NCBJ) says hackers targeted its IT infrastructure, but the attack was detected and blocked before causing any impact. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Betterleaks, a new open-source secrets scanner to replace Gitleaks
item.description:
A new open-source tool called Betterleaks can scan directories, files, and git repositories and identify valid secrets using default or customized rules. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Unknown
item.description:
Unknown reported a healthcare breach.
📰 Source: HHS OCR | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach
